title: Macramé ladder shelf
subtitle: A two-tier hanging shelf with knotted side panels and wooden shelf boards
excerpt: Knot a two-tier hanging shelf with macramé side supports and two slim wooden boards for shelves. The shelf uses square knots and gathering knots for the side panels; the boards sit in the knot structure without additional fixing. The finished piece hangs about 60 cm long and each shelf holds a modest weight of books or plants.
sourceNotes: Synthesised from traditional macramé knotting practice. Macramé shelf supports are documented in 1970s British and American craft manuals and in contemporary macramé teaching.

glossaryTerms.definition: A flat, symmetrical knot made with two working cords around two filler cords. Used in the side panels and the section between the two shelves.
A wrapping knot made by coiling a single cord many times tightly around a bundle of cords. Used here to cinch the cords just below each shelf board and to form the upper hanging loop.
A folded-cord mounting knot. Used to attach all cords to the top holding ring or dowel at the start of this project.

content.text: for the decorative side panels and
to hold the shelf boards in place and to form the top hanging loop. The shelf boards sit across the cords in slots formed by the knot structure;
at the top give the whole piece a clean starting edge.

items.name: Wooden boards or timber slices, 30 cm by 15 cm (two boards for two shelves)
Copper or wooden ring, 5 cm, for the top hanging loop
Small drill and 6 mm drill bit for pilot holes in the shelf boards

content.text: Drill two 6 mm holes in each shelf board, one at each corner along the long side, each hole 2 cm in from the edge. The cords will thread through these holes to support the shelf. Sand the boards smooth and oil or wax them if desired before knotting.
Step 2: Mount cords on the ring'

Cut 8 cords each 6 metres long. Fold each cord in half and mount on the top ring using lark's head knots. Divide the 16 cord ends into two groups of 8: the left-side support and the right-side support. Each group of 8 will form one side of the shelf structure.
Step 3: Knot to the first shelf'

On each side group, work alternating square knots for 15 cm below the ring. Then thread 4 cords from each side group through the drilled holes on the first shelf board: 2 cords through each hole. Below the shelf board, work a gathering knot on each side to hold the board firmly in place.
Step 4: Knot to the second shelf'

Continue with alternating square knots for another 15 cm below the first shelf gathering knots. Thread the same cords through the second shelf board holes and work another gathering knot below to hold the second board in place.

Continue alternating square knots for 10 cm below the second shelf, then gather all the cords from both sides into one group and work a large gathering knot around all cords 5 cm from the last square knots. Trim the cord tails below the gathering knot to a 10 cm fringe, or tie each cord in an overhand knot and trim.

items.problem: The shelf boards tilt rather than sitting level
items.cause: The cord lengths on one side are longer than on the other due to uneven knotting tension
items.fix: Before mounting the first board, check both side groups are the same length from the ring. If they differ, add or subtract a few knots on the shorter side to equalise before threading the board.

items.problem: The gathering knot under the shelf board slips down the cord over time
items.cause: Gathering knot was not pulled tight enough around all cords
items.fix: Make the gathering knot with very firm, even coils and secure the tail end by threading it back up through the coil with a needle before trimming.
